mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically boosting present valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment acceleration signal.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between housing starts and revolving consumer credit indicates a structural shift in demand composition; when housing starts fall while credit balances rise, it signals debt-fueled consumption that precedes a demand shock and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns when HOUST declines and RSAFS increases simultaneously, with Rank IC > -0.05.

mechanism_narrative: In a supply-constrained environment, rising industrial production reflects pricing power rather than demand overheating, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as corporate margins expand.
regime_hint: supply-constrained expansion with sticky input costs
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns turn negative when INDPRO rises above 108 during periods of elevated input cost inflation, breaking the margin expansion premise.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP directly correlates with earnings revision momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as institutional capital flows into confirmed growth trajectories.
failure_condition: Walk-forward correlation between GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth and 5-day SPX returns drops below 0.10 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, the cost of capital suppresses equity valuations and corporate investment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive or neutral in > 50% of observations where FEDFUNDS sustains levels above 4.5% for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of nonfarm payrolls and the unemployment rate determines labor market tightness; when PAYEMS growth decelerates while UNRATE remains elevated above 5.0%, it signals a cooling labor market that reduces consumer spending power and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS deceleration and UNRATE > 5.0% signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns, with forward return median exceeding 0%.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, rising consumer credit balances indicate pent-up demand being released, which positively impacts corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with accommodative credit conditions
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns turn negative when RSAFS rises during periods of tightening credit spreads, invalidating the pent-up demand transmission channel.
